result.summary: Found two non-blocking issues in the one-file diff. I could not run tests because `pytest` and `uv` are not installed in this environment; `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` passed.

findings.title: Generated correlation token is never exposed to the client
findings.body: The new per-request token is logged server-side, but `/move` still returns only `move_uci` and does not set a response header or accept a client-supplied request id. That means the stated client-side correlation use case cannot work: the client has no token to match against server logs.
findings.recommendation: Return the same value in a response header such as `X-Request-ID`, or accept and log a client-provided request id. Add an API test asserting the correlation id is visible to the caller.

findings.title: Token generation consumes the same global RNG used for move selection
findings.body: `_new_engine_session_token()` calls `random.choice` 16 times immediately before `pick_random_legal_move(board)`, which also defaults to the module-global `random`. This couples request-id generation to engine move selection and makes seeded/debug replay behavior depend on an unrelated logging detail.
findings.recommendation: Generate correlation ids with an RNG independent of gameplay randomness, for example `secrets.token_urlsafe()` or `uuid.uuid4()`, then leave `random_move` as the only consumer of the gameplay RNG.

next_steps: Install the project test runner or run in the CI image, then execute `pytest src/engine/playlike_engine -q`.
Add coverage for whichever request-correlation contract is intended: response header, response body field, or client-supplied request id.
